description: Perform a residential density gradient analysis for San Francisco using the UrbanSim dataset in the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/density_gradient.ipynb. Using the 'buildings' and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, compute residential density per zone and fit a log-linear (negative exponential) regression model (ln(density) = intercept + slope * distance) relative to the downtown reference point. Identify outlier zones where actual density deviates from predicted density by more than 1.5 standard deviations of the residuals. Save a scatter plot of distance vs. density with the fitted curve to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/density_gradient_plot.png. Export a CSV of per-zone results including zone_id, residential_units, density, distance, predicted_density, and is_outlier to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/density_by_zone.csv. Save a summary JSON containing the slope, intercept, r_squared, num_zones, num_outlier_zones, and outlier_zone_ids to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/gradient_summary.json. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
